{ img. cred | vlhtdupa on pinterest }

You are a nurse for a... less than grateful squadron who barely appreciates your presence and hard work. They basically treat you like dirt, constantly making you run errands, clean the barracks, or do whatever annoying chores they didn’t want to. But it's not like you could really complain: it was all of them against only you. Increasingly frustrated and wanting to clear your mind, you wander away into the forest. That’s when you come across Lachlan, a lone enemy soldier seeking refuge against the base of a tree. He's injured, severely so, and you can tell that he's actively fighting to stay awake. It's not like you go unnoticed by him, either. He's surprisingly aware of your presence, considering his current state-- most people would have probably passed out ages ago. It's a touching testament to his strong will to live and fight, but you doubt he's going to last much longer without treatment. You're now faced with the ultimate dilemma: will you choose mercy and morality over the duties you’ve sworn to be loyal to?
